# R2.01 · Atomic updates

> Read-modify-write without a race. Redis offers three atomic tools — WATCH/MULTI/EXEC optimistic locking (the
> contrast), Lua scripts for complex logic, and shadow-key + RENAME for safe bulk swaps. Grounded in EchoMQ's real
> atomic moves: every state transition is one inline Lua script declared with `EchoMQ.Script.new/2` and run
> EVALSHA-first by `EchoMQ.Connector.eval/5` — `EchoMQ.Jobs.enqueue/4` (`@enqueue`: kind check, EXISTS dup refusal,
> HSET the row, ZADD pending, atomic) and `enqueue_many/3` (SCRIPT LOAD once + pipelined EVALSHA). Served at
> /redis-patterns/coordination/atomic-updates.

## Pages

- [Hub](/redis-patterns/coordination/atomic-updates): the pattern and its three dives.
- [WATCH / MULTI / EXEC](/redis-patterns/coordination/atomic-updates/watch-multi-exec): optimistic locking — the generic check-and-set that aborts and retries when a watched key changes before EXEC; echo_mq replaced it with one Lua EVAL.
- [Lua for logic](/redis-patterns/coordination/atomic-updates/lua-for-logic): the whole transition as one inline Lua script that never interleaves — the real `@claim`/`@complete`/`@retry`; EVALSHA → NOSCRIPT → SCRIPT LOAD dispatch.
- [Shadow key & bulk](/redis-patterns/coordination/atomic-updates/shadow-key-bulk): build the new value in a temp key then atomic RENAME; `EchoMQ.Jobs.enqueue_many/3` pipelines EVALSHA for all-or-each batches.

## Up

- [R2 · Coordination](/redis-patterns/coordination) · [Course home](/redis-patterns)
